cards.back: A language that is easier for humans to read and write.
A language close to machine code that gives direct hardware control.

cards.front: When might a low-level language be used?
cards.back: When high performance or close hardware control is required.
They are easier and faster for humans to develop and maintain.

cards.front: What is the main difference between high- and low-level languages?
cards.back: High-level languages are more human-readable, while low-level languages are closer to machine code.
